data.title: Iranian media: Iran plans to pay transit fees for the Strait of Hormuz through Iranian banks.
data.description: According to the Iranian Students' News Agency, parliamentary officials stated that the tolls Iran plans to levy on the Strait of Hormuz will be paid through Iranian banks. (Jinshi)

data.title: Syria Begins Loading Iraqi Oil for Export Amid Strait Closure
data.description: Syria's oil company announced on the 15th that the first batch of Iraqi 're-exported' oil began loading at the Baniyas port refinery. According to Odaily, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has hindered Iraq's oil exports, making transit through Syria a viable alternative. Ahmed Kubaji, the deputy director of Syria's oil company, stated at a press conference that under the agreement between Syria and Iraq, Iraqi oil is transported by truck to the Baniyas refinery, then transferred to the port's oil terminal for sea export. The first tanker will load approximately 500,000 tons of oil, with the loading operation expected to take at least three days. Kubaji noted that Syria is entitled to extract the necessary oil for its power plants from the Iraqi shipments, with the remainder designated for export.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,300 USDT with a 1.16% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 16, 2026, 13:55 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,300 USDT and is now trading at 2,293.570068 USDT, with a narrowed 1.16% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Israeli Military Prepares for Potential Ceasefire with Lebanon
data.description: The Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) have announced their readiness for a potential ceasefire with Lebanon. According to NS3.AI, military officials have informed troops that the ceasefire could commence between 19:00 local time on the 16th and midnight. Senior commanders have been directed to prepare the forces currently stationed in southern Lebanon for this possible halt in hostilities.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Gold Dips to $4,790 per Ounce
data.description: Spot gold prices have decreased to $4,790 per ounce, marking a 0.07% decline for the day. According to Jin10, this movement reflects ongoing market fluctuations in the precious metals sector.

data.title: The first shipment of Iraqi oil, transiting through Syria, has begun loading.
data.description: The Syrian Oil Company (SML) announced on the 15th that the first batch of Iraqi "re-exported" oil began loading at the Baniyas port refinery in Syria. Due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, Iraqi oil exports have been hampered, making transshipment via Syria an alternative. SML Deputy Director Ahmed Kubagi stated at a press conference on the 15th that, according to an agreement between Syria and Iraq, Iraqi oil will first be transported by truck to the Baniyas refinery, then transferred to the port terminal, and subsequently exported by sea. The first tanker will carry approximately 500,000 tons of oil, and the loading process will take at least three days. Kubagi added that, according to the agreement, Syria can extract the amount of oil needed for its power plants from the Iraqi shipments, with the remainder being exported. (CCTV News)

data.title: Charles Schwab officially launches spot cryptocurrency trading service
data.description: Charles Schwab officially launched its Schwab Crypto spot cryptocurrency trading service on April 16, which will be rolled out to retail investors in phases over the next few weeks. The service supports direct trading of Bitcoin and Ethereum, and includes educational content and professional customer support, while integrating with Schwab's existing investment, trading, wealth management, and banking services.

data.title: Schwab Introduces Crypto Trading Service for Retail Clients
data.description: Schwab has announced the launch of its Schwab Crypto service, offering spot cryptocurrency trading. According to Foresight News, the service will be rolled out to retail clients in phases over the coming weeks. Schwab Crypto will provide direct trading channels for Bitcoin and Ethereum, along with educational content and professional support.

data.title: HYPE Whale Recovers Losses and Gains Profit Amid Price Surge
data.description:
On April 16, a significant development was observed in the cryptocurrency market. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, a major investor, who had previously faced substantial losses, has now recovered and gained profits due to a surge in the price of HYPE.

Last November, the investor opened a long position on HYPE at its peak price of $38.6. However, the value of HYPE subsequently declined, reaching a low of $20.5 by the end of January, resulting in a substantial unrealized loss of $26 million.

Currently, the price of HYPE has increased by 120% from its lowest point of $20.5. This rise has allowed the investor to not only recover from the previous losses but also achieve an unrealized profit of $8.5 million.

data.title: Soluna Holdings Acquires Full Ownership of AI Compute Project
data.description: Soluna Holdings, a Nasdaq-listed company, has announced the acquisition of Spring Lane Capital's stake in the AI compute project, Project Dorothy 1A (D1A), for $16.5 million, securing 100% ownership. According to ChainCatcher, earlier this month, Soluna Holdings also acquired the Briscoe Wind Farm for $53 million. The company plans to accelerate the transformation of its integrated compute base, focusing on AI computing.

data.title: JST Burns 271 Million Tokens in Deflationary Move
data.description:
JST has recently executed a significant token burn, destroying 271 million JST tokens, which represents 2.74% of the current total supply. According to ChainCatcher, this burn is valued at approximately $21.3 million. The treasury reserve has now surpassed the $100 million mark, providing a solid foundation for the continuation of the deflationary process.

The funds for the buyback and burn were sourced from the protocol's actual revenue, establishing an automated deflationary cycle driven by real income. This development indicates that JST is steadily progressing towards a sustainable economic model with long-term viability.

data.title: A whale opened a long position of 1.38 million HYPE tokens, broke even after initial losses, and realized a profit of $8.5 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, a whale opened a long position of 1.38 million HYPE tokens at the November high of $38.6, worth $62.13 million. At that time, HYPE fell to a low of $20.5 at the end of January, resulting in a paper loss of $26 million. Since then, the price of HYPE has rebounded to $45, a 120% increase from the low, turning the whale's paper loss into a break-even point and realizing a profit of $8.5 million.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Sahara AI Launches Sorin Investment Agent for Comprehensive Asset Management
data.description: Sahara AI has officially launched its investment agent, Sorin, which supports research, analysis, and trading execution across cryptocurrencies, stocks, prediction markets, and tokenized assets. According to Foresight News, Sorin can be deployed locally on desktop for autonomous trading, ensuring non-custodial operations without fund locking. Previously, 20,000 early users participated in testing. Sorin now offers features such as automated execution of quantitative strategies, real-time cross-market monitoring, and personalized risk management, which were previously exclusive to institutional investors, to all users.

data.title: Hong Kong's Stablecoin Licensing Strategy Unveiled
data.description:
Hong Kong has issued its first batch of stablecoin licenses, marking a significant development in the region's financial landscape. According to PANews, traditional banks have emerged as the primary recipients of these licenses, while Web3 players have taken a backseat in this regulatory move. This strategic decision highlights Hong Kong's approach to integrating stablecoins into its financial system, prioritizing established banking institutions over newer digital entities.

The issuance of stablecoin licenses is part of Hong Kong's broader plan to regulate digital currencies and ensure financial stability. By granting licenses to traditional banks, the region aims to leverage their existing infrastructure and expertise in managing financial risks. This move is seen as a way to balance innovation with security, providing a controlled environment for stablecoin operations.

Web3 companies, which have been at the forefront of digital currency innovation, are now observing from the sidelines as traditional banks take the lead. This shift underscores the challenges faced by newer players in navigating regulatory frameworks designed for conventional financial institutions.

Hong Kong's stablecoin licensing strategy reflects its commitment to maintaining a robust financial system while embracing digital advancements. As the region continues to develop its regulatory approach, the impact on both traditional and digital financial sectors will be closely watched.

data.title: Naver and Dunamu Plan IPO Committee for Naver Financial Following Share Swap
data.description: Naver and Dunamu have announced plans to establish an IPO committee for Naver Financial within a year following their share swap agreement, according to a corrected filing. The companies aim to list Naver Financial within five years, with a possible two-year extension. However, Dunamu has stated that no specific decisions have been made regarding the timing or structure of the IPO. The approximately $10.3 billion all-stock deal is pending regulatory approval.

data.title: The Israeli military says it is ready for a ceasefire with Lebanon.
data.description: According to an Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) official, the IDF is prepared to reach a ceasefire agreement with Lebanon, as reported on the 16th local time. The official stated that senior IDF commanders were instructed to prepare troops currently deployed in southern Lebanon for a ceasefire. They were informed that the ceasefire would begin between 7 PM and midnight local time on the 16th. (CCTV News)

data.title: Sahara AI's full-asset investment agent, Sorin, has been officially launched.
data.description: On April 16th, Sahara AI officially launched its full-asset investment agent, Sorin. Sorin supports research, analysis, and trade execution in cryptocurrencies, stocks, prediction markets, and tokenized assets. The desktop version allows for local deployment and autonomous trading, with no custodianship or fund lock-up. It has already completed testing with 20,000 early users. Capabilities previously limited to institutional investors, such as automated quantitative strategy execution, real-time cross-market monitoring, and personalized risk management, are now available to all users through Sorin.

data.title: US stocks opened with mixed results for the crypto sector, with Solana (HSDT) rising over 1%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the opening of the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.14%, the S&P 500 rose 0.15%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.18%. The crypto sector showed mixed performance, with Solana rising 1.24%, Circle rising 0.21%, and SharpLink rising 0.13%; while Strategy (MSTR) fell 0.91%, Bitmine fell 0.18%, and Coinbase fell 0.06%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: U.S. Unemployment Claims Lower Than Expected, Fed Rate Unchanged Likely
data.description:
The number of initial unemployment claims in the United States for the week ending April 11 was reported at 207,000, falling short of the anticipated 215,000. According to BlockBeats, the previous figure was revised from 219,000 to 218,000.

Data from CME's FedWatch indicates that following the release of the unemployment claims data, there is a 99.5% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain interest rates in April, with only a 0.5% chance of a 25 basis point increase.

data.title: Ethereum Declines 1.3% Amid Mixed Performance in CoinDesk 20 Index
data.description: Ethereum experienced a 1.3% decline as the CoinDesk 20 Index recorded a slight decrease, trading at 2,083.34, down 0.2% since 4 p.m. ET on Wednesday. According to NS3.AI, the index showed mixed performance with twelve out of the twenty assets trading higher.
